title: Misatos Discovery in 25 Adam Lilith Humans
display_title: Misatos Discovery in 25 Adam Lilith Humans
wikitext:
[]: Misato's Discovery in 25' [Adam/Lilith, Humans]             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Thu Mar 17, 2011 9:06 pm Perhaps this is because I'm using a fansubbed version as opposed to the official one, but while rewatching EoE I noticed what seem to be two blatant contradictions in one scene. Misato is telling Shinji the truth about 2I and she says "mankind was spawned from a being called Lilith, just like Adam was ". Wait, what? Adam is a seed of life, it didn't come from Lilith. She then says, "the other angels are just different possibilities of what we could have become". But again, this doesn't make any sense. The other angels are not possibilities of what humans could have become, because they are Adam's children, not Lilith's. After all, humans don't have the fruit of life, and angels don't have the fruit of knowledge. Hence the whole idea of "forbidden fusion" and whatnot. Does Misato simply not understand the difference between Adam and Lilith, or is there something I'm missing here?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Mar 17, 2011 9:10 pm It's ADV's fuck-up, as far as I know. The second line is correctly translated, although it betrays that Misato's notion of "human" is about as vague as possible. The first is not; it should be something more like "Mankind was spawned from a being called Lilith, who is like Adam."

[]: Re: Misato's Discovery in 25'             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 17, 2011 9:28 pm rhfxz1s wrote: "the other angels are just different possibilities of what we could have become".

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Thu Mar 17, 2011 9:56 pm So does NGE essentially consider a "human" to be the endpoint or desired result of evolution? That is, homo sapiens are the final result of Lilith's blood, and the Angels are the final results of Adam's respectively? Sorry if that doesn't make any sense; it was worded weird but I couldn't think of any other way to say it.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Mar 17, 2011 10:12 pm Anything created by a Seed of Life is "human" - the Seeds themselves count as well. So that includes everything from Homo sapiens and Angels down to trees and shit.

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Thu Mar 17, 2011 10:43 pm Well that's silly. When you put it that way every living thing becomes a human. (with the exception of FAR, I suppose) Thanks for clearing it up though.

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Thu Mar 17, 2011 10:45 pm So Pen-Pen's a Lilin?

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Mar 17, 2011 10:51 pm COACH wrote: So Pen-Pen's a Lilin?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Mar 18, 2011 1:13 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Mar 18, 2011 1:19 am Ah yeah, that's right, I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 18, 2011 1:35 am Azathoth wrote: Anything created by a Seed of Life is "human" - the Seeds themselves count as well. So that includes everything from Homo sapiens and Angels down to trees and shit.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Mar 18, 2011 12:37 pm Reichu wrote: That makes "human" pretty meaningless, doesn't it? You sure you're not doing it wrong? :p

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 18, 2011 1:05 pm All life in NGE has souls, but this isn't the same thing as all life having souls that trace to the progenitor humans. For all we know, the FAR souls sat around doing nothing until a humanoid species had evolved to receive them. Whatever the case, NGE's anthropocentric sci-fi conceit is operating under the principle that humans are somehow special.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Mar 18, 2011 1:13 pm Then where do the souls in question come from? Did the FAR bring along the souls of trees from Cybertron and have Lilith see to it that they got reincarnated as trees here? actually that is astoundingly plausible given the general lunacy of the FAR I do agree that humans are special in NGE - after all, some plants have regenerated within moments of Rei's death in EoE. It makes sense that plants, which don't have any fears or sufferings as we understand them, wouldn't hesitate to return to life. Where's the line drawn, though? There are certainly animals which experience suffering and fear. What makes humans in NGE different?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Mar 18, 2011 2:55 pm The growing population and lack of reincarnation indicates that souls can be created somehow, so...trees have souls. Humans = Lilin/Angels =/= Other Lilith-derived life because Human = conscious and capable of higher reasoning, rationality, and emotion. Animals and such just go "derpderp must eat must poop must fuck." There are certainly animals which experience suffering and fear. What makes humans in NGE different?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Mar 18, 2011 3:00 pm AT, I think you're giving animals too little credit and humans too much.

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Fri Mar 18, 2011 3:33 pm Could it be said that anything with a Fruit is considered a 'human' in NGE? Granted, Evas don't inherently have Fruits ( I think ...), but they're synthetic humans, so that might not matter.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 18, 2011 4:13 pm COACH wrote: Could it be said that anything with a Fruit is considered a 'human' in NGE?

[]:             Quote Post by Sik » Wed Mar 30, 2011 12:16 am Then where do the souls in question come from? Did the FAR bring along the souls of trees from Cybertron and have Lilith see to it that they got reincarnated as trees here?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Mar 30, 2011 1:33 pm Pretty much, yea. Though it is mentioned in that thar supplemental material that the FAR's souls were reincarnated in the Chamber of Guf. The original humans on this planet are probably former FAR and stuff, along with the Angels, but they wouldn't remember so it doesn't seem to matter. It also seems that new souls can be made when two people bump uglies.

Misato is telling Shinji the truth about 2I and she says "mankind was spawned from a being called Lilith, just like Adam was ". Wait, what?
